title: Carrying Capacity
excerpt: Carrying capacity is the amount of weight a creature can normally carry. It is based on Strength and can be altered by size or other effects.
markdown:
# Carrying Capacity

Carrying capacity is the amount of weight a creature can carry as a normal load. This rule becomes relevant when the world-state needs an actual weight limit instead of treating gear and treasure abstractly.

## Core Rule

A creature's carrying capacity equals its Strength score multiplied by 15. The result is the amount of weight, in pounds, that the creature can carry.

## Pushing, Dragging, And Lifting

A creature can push, drag, or lift up to twice its carrying capacity. This is the same as 30 times the creature's Strength score in pounds.

## State Modifiers

- Size can change how much weight a creature can manage
- Specific effects, traits, or forms can increase or decrease carrying capacity
- When one of those rules applies, use the modified capacity for later load checks

## Resolution Meaning

Most ordinary loads do not need constant weight accounting. Carrying capacity matters when a creature tries to haul something unusually heavy, move while burdened by extreme load, or carry another creature, object, or amount of treasure that pushes past normal assumptions.
